description: Replaces exact phrase matching with OR-expansion for multi-token queries to fix zero-result failures.
rationale: Current phrase wrapping causes 19 of 30 eval queries to return zero results because multi-word topics rarely appear as exact phrases. OR-expansion trades phrase precision for dramatically higher recall.
expected_improvements: Multi-word topic queries (e.g., "autoresearch projects", "railway deploy issues") should find results instead of returning zero. Bag-of-words matching will surface messages containing any of the query terms.
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -114,17 +114,15 @@ def health():
 # =============================================================
 # QUERY FUNCTIONS — shared by REST endpoints and MCP tools
 # =============================================================
 
 def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
-    """Wrap bare multi-token queries in quotes so BM25 ranks positional
-    phrase matches instead of bag-of-words token density. Callers passing
-    explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, booleans, NEAR, prefix*, column filters,
-    grouping) pass through unchanged. Also makes hyphenated tokens work
-    without requiring callers to quote them themselves."""
+    """Expand bare multi-token queries to OR terms for broad recall instead
+    of forcing exact phrase match. Single-token queries and explicit FTS5
+    syntax (quotes, operators, wildcards) pass through unchanged."""
     if not q or any(c in q for c in '"*():^'):
         return q
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    return ' OR '.join(tokens) if len(tokens) > 1 else q
 
 
 def query_search(q: str, limit: int = 20, type: str = None, workspace_id: str = None):

score_reasons.retrieval: MRR improved by 0.18 and 11 queries fixed, but 1 query regressed (cross-session category), triggering the regression penalty cap.
score_reasons.minimalism: Net negative two lines, removes phrase-wrapping complexity, replaces with simpler OR-expansion that dramatically improves recall.
score_reasons.reliability: Preserves all FTS5 syntax passthrough guards, handles single-token and multi-token cases correctly, no new failure modes introduced.
score_reasons.taste: Clean idiomatic code, precise description naming exact mechanism, clear docstring, matches existing style and patterns perfectly.

description: Add prefix wildcards to query tokens for morphological matching without stemming.
rationale: FTS5 prefix matching catches variants (deploy→deployment, run→running) without schema changes. Adding `*` to tokens ≥3 chars gives stemming-like behavior at zero cost.
expected_improvements: Technical term queries ("deploy autoresearch", "running code") should match morphological variants where exact forms don't exist in the corpus.
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -114,18 +114,16 @@ def health():
 # QUERY FUNCTIONS — shared by REST endpoints and MCP tools
 # =============================================================
 
 def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
-    """Wrap bare multi-token queries in quotes so BM25 ranks positional
-    phrase matches instead of bag-of-words token density. Callers passing
-    explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, booleans, NEAR, prefix*, column filters,
-    grouping) pass through unchanged. Also makes hyphenated tokens work
-    without requiring callers to quote them themselves."""
+    """Add prefix wildcards and OR-expand to match morphological variants.
+    Preserves explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, operators, wildcards)."""
     if not q or any(c in q for c in '"*():^'):
         return q
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    expanded = [f"{t}*" if len(t) >= 3 else t for t in tokens]
+    return " OR ".join(expanded)
 
 
 def query_search(q: str, limit: int = 20, type: str = None, workspace_id: str = None):

score_reasons.retrieval: MRR increased 0.23, precision and recall both improved, 11 queries fixed, zero real regressions.

description: Add Porter stemmer to FTS5 tokenizer to normalize word forms at index time
rationale: Stemming normalizes inflections (deploying→deploy, improvements→improve) so queries match morphological variants without prefix wildcards. This is a standard FTS5 feature with proven effectiveness.
expected_improvements: Queries with different word forms will match (deployed/deploying/deployment), reducing zero-result failures on morphological variants. Technical terms and verb forms benefit most.

   content,
   content=messages,
-  content_rowid=id
+  content_rowid=id,
+  tokenize='porter unicode61'
 );

description: Replace phrase-wrapping with OR-expansion for multi-token queries to fix zero-result failures.
rationale: The current phrase-only strategy forces exact matches that rarely exist in conversation data, causing 19 of 30 queries to fail. OR-expansion lets BM25 rank documents by term coverage instead of requiring exact phrase sequences.
expected_improvements: Multi-word topic queries like 'autoresearch projects ranked' will match messages containing any of those terms instead of returning zero results. Queries with explicit syntax (quotes, wildcards, operators) pass through unchanged.
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -132,18 +132,17 @@
 # QUERY FUNCTIONS — shared by REST endpoints and MCP tools
 # =============================================================
 
 def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
-    """Wrap bare multi-token queries in quotes so BM25 ranks positional
-    phrase matches instead of bag-of-words token density. Callers passing
-    explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, booleans, NEAR, prefix*, column filters,
-    grouping) pass through unchanged. Also makes hyphenated tokens work
-    without requiring callers to quote them themselves."""
+    """Convert bare multi-token queries to OR-expansion for better recall.
+    Single tokens and explicit FTS5 syntax pass through unchanged."""
     if not q or any(c in q for c in '"*():^'):
         return q
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    if len(tokens) == 1:
+        return q
+    return " OR ".join(tokens)

expected_improvements: Multi-word topic queries improve via OR-expansion, morphological variants improve via prefix matching ("deploy" matches "deployment"), partial technical terms improve ("autoresearch" matches "autoresearch").

description: Combine phrase matching with OR-expansion fallback to boost precision for adjacent token occurrences while preserving recall
rationale: Patches 1 and 2 provide excellent recall via OR and prefix wildcards but treat all token arrangements equally. When tokens appear as an exact phrase in the corpus, those messages are inherently more relevant—the hybrid query ranks them higher while still matching scattered occurrences.
expected_improvements: Multi-word topic queries like "railway deploy" or "cloud context" where the exact phrase exists in some messages will rank those higher, while still returning partial matches. Improves precision without sacrificing recall.
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -148,7 +148,16 @@ def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    
+    # Single token: prefix wildcard for morphological variants
+    if len(tokens) == 1:
+        return f"{tokens[0]}*" if len(tokens[0]) >= 3 else tokens[0]
+    
+    # Multi-token: phrase match (boosts adjacent occurrences) + OR fallback (maintains recall)
+    phrase = " ".join(tokens)
+    expanded = [f"{t}*" if len(t) >= 3 else t for t in tokens]
+    or_clause = " OR ".join(expanded)
+    return f'"{phrase}" OR ({or_clause})'
 
 
 def query_search(q: str, limit: int = 20, type: str = None, workspace_id: str = None):

score_reasons.minimalism: Adds 9 net lines within single function with reasonable branching complexity but no new abstractions or dependencies.
score_reasons.reliability: Preserves FTS5 passthrough guards, handles edge cases correctly, compound query syntax valid, no new failure modes.
score_reasons.taste: Matches existing code style, clear variable names, precise description references exact mechanism, no marketing fluff.

description: Combines NEAR proximity matching with OR fallback to rank semantically coherent results higher while maintaining broad coverage
rationale: Pure phrase matching returns zero results for most multi-word queries. Pure OR-expansion has high recall but poor precision, ranking scattered term matches equally with semantically coherent passages. NEAR+OR provides proximity-based ranking with bag-of-words fallback, ensuring no zero-result failures while surfacing semantically relevant matches first.
expected_improvements: Multi-word topic queries like "railway deploy" or "autoresearch projects" should rank messages where terms appear close together higher than messages where terms are scattered, improving top-result precision while maintaining recall
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -105,18 +105,24 @@
 # QUERY FUNCTIONS — shared by REST endpoints and MCP tools
 # =============================================================
 
 def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
-    """Wrap bare multi-token queries in quotes so BM25 ranks positional
-    phrase matches instead of bag-of-words token density. Callers passing
-    explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, booleans, NEAR, prefix*, column filters,
-    grouping) pass through unchanged. Also makes hyphenated tokens work
-    without requiring callers to quote them themselves."""
+    """Combine NEAR proximity matching with OR fallback. NEAR ranks terms
+    appearing close together higher while OR ensures broad coverage. Prefix
+    wildcards handle morphological variants. Preserves explicit FTS5 syntax."""
     if not q or any(c in q for c in '"*():^'):
         return q
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    
+    prefixed = [f"{t}*" for t in tokens]
+    if len(prefixed) == 1:
+        return prefixed[0]
+    
+    # NEAR for precision, OR for recall
+    near = f"NEAR({' '.join(prefixed)}, 10)"
+    bag = " OR ".join(prefixed)
+    return f"({near} OR {bag})"
 
 
 def query_search(q: str, limit: int = 20, type: str = None, workspace_id: str = None):

score_reasons.minimalism: Adds 6 net lines with NEAR+OR+prefix logic; reasonable complexity for the gain but not minimal.
score_reasons.reliability: Preserves all FTS5 passthrough checks, handles edge cases robustly, dramatically reduces zero-result failures.
score_reasons.taste: Clean code, precise description naming NEAR and OR mechanisms, excellent rationale, matches existing style.

description: Applies prefix expansion to both NEAR proximity and OR fallback clauses to catch morphological variants while preserving proximity-based ranking
rationale: Patch 5 NEAR+OR ranked proximity but missed variants (deploy vs deployed). Adding prefix expansion to both clauses catches morphological variants within proximity context, so 'deploy railway' matches 'deployed to railway' ranked by adjacency.
expected_improvements: Queries with morphological variants near target terms (e.g., 'deploy railway' → 'deployed to railway', 'bug fix' → 'bugs fixed'). Maintains NEAR proximity ranking while expanding term coverage.
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -146,18 +146,28 @@ def health():
 # QUERY FUNCTIONS — shared by REST endpoints and MCP tools
 # =============================================================
 
 def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
-    """Wrap bare multi-token queries in quotes so BM25 ranks positional
-    phrase matches instead of bag-of-words token density. Callers passing
-    explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, booleans, NEAR, prefix*, column filters,
-    grouping) pass through unchanged. Also makes hyphenated tokens work
-    without requiring callers to quote them themselves."""
+    """Expand bare queries with NEAR proximity + OR fallback and prefix matching.
+    
+    Single tokens get prefix expansion (term → term*). Multi-word queries become
+    (term1* NEAR term2* NEAR term3*) OR term1* OR term2* OR term3*, ranking
+    proximity matches higher while maintaining broad recall through OR fallback.
+    
+    Callers passing explicit FTS5 syntax (quotes, booleans, NEAR, prefix*,
+    column filters, grouping) pass through unchanged."""
     if not q or any(c in q for c in '"*():^'):
         return q
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    
+    prefixed_tokens = [f"{t}*" if len(t) >= 3 else t for t in tokens]
+    if len(tokens) == 1:
+        return prefixed_tokens[0]
+    
+    near_query = " NEAR ".join(prefixed_tokens)
+    or_query = " OR ".join(prefixed_tokens)
+    return f"({near_query}) OR {or_query}"
 
 
 def query_search(q: str, limit: int = 20, type: str = None, workspace_id: str = None):

description: Apply prefix expansion selectively to OR fallback terms (length >= 4) while keeping NEAR clause precise
rationale: Patch 2 showed prefix helps OR expansion (+0.045 MRR vs pure OR), but patch 5 showed applying prefix to NEAR degrades performance (-0.034 MRR vs NEAR-only). Limiting prefix to OR fallback and longer tokens should capture morphological variants without adding noise to proximity matching.
expected_improvements: Multi-word queries with morphological variants like "deploy" vs "deployment" should match via prefixed OR terms while NEAR maintains precision for proximity-based ranking. Short tokens avoid over-matching noise.

--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py

     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    near_clause = f'NEAR({" ".join(tokens)}, 10)'
+    or_terms = [f"{t}*" if len(t) >= 4 else t for t in tokens]
+    or_clause = " OR ".join(or_terms)
+    return f'{near_clause} OR {or_clause}'

description: Combines exact phrase matching with AND-prefix fallback in a single FTS5 query using OR operator.
rationale: Single-pass query avoids multiple database round trips while giving BM25 both exact phrase (highest rank) and AND-prefix (fallback) scoring in one MATCH operation. BM25 naturally ranks exact phrase matches higher than AND-prefix matches.
expected_improvements: Multi-word topic queries return results more reliably (phrase match for precision, AND-prefix fallback for recall). Single-token queries gain prefix expansion for morphological variants.
unified_diff:
--- a/engine/server.py
+++ b/engine/server.py
@@ -150,7 +150,14 @@ def _auto_phrase(q: str) -> str:
     tokens = q.split()
     if any(op in tokens for op in ("AND", "OR", "NOT", "NEAR")):
         return q
-    return f'"{q}"'
+    
+    if len(tokens) == 1:
+        return f"{q}*" if len(q) >= 3 else q
+    
+    prefix_terms = [f"{t}*" if len(t) >= 3 else t for t in tokens]
+    phrase = f'"{q}"'
+    expanded = " AND ".join(prefix_terms)
+    return f"{phrase} OR ({expanded})"
 
 
 def query_search(q: str, limit: int = 20, type: str = None, workspace_id: str = None):

score_reasons.retrieval: MRR improved 0.1667 (large delta), 7 queries improved vs 1 regressed, strong gains in topic_search and error_lookup categories.
score_reasons.minimalism: Net +7 lines, modifies _auto_phrase in-place, straightforward OR logic with prefix expansion, no new functions.
score_reasons.reliability: Preserves FTS5 passthrough, handles single and multi-token cases, length guards prevent overly broad prefix matching.
score_reasons.taste: Clean code matching existing style, precise description naming OR mechanism, zero anti-pattern buzzwords, readable variable names.
